rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DAVID K BALL
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required, as nothing has been filed to address the following issues (last updated and e-mailed to counsel on 12/27/2023 - see also tentative rulings for the past 3 hearings): 1) No additional inventory and appraisal has been filed since the partial one only listing small cash was filed in March 2023. 2) Has the petitioner taken possession of all the securities appraised by the referee in June 2022? If not, please describe efforts the petitioner has made and other updates since her supplemental declaration filed in February 2023. 3) Please provide an itemized list of assets escheated to the state and sought to be recovered. 4) Has the petitioner taken any IAEA actions reportable under CRC 7.250(a)? 5) Each status report must set forth all the information required by LRSF 14.35(J)(1), even if it was included in previous reports. Allegations re notice under Probate Code section 9202 and assignments or powers of attorney that may affect distribution must be provided. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HIDEO IWATA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/6/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (2) The calculation of the statutory compensation does not meet the requirements of Probate Code Section 10800(b) and 10810(b) or CRC 7.705. (The statutory fee base is not the value of assets on hand at the time of distribution.) (3) Who is the successor-in-interest to the post-deceased heir? Has a personal representative been appointed to administer his estate? Did he have a trust? [Probate Code Section 11802 and LRSF 14.35 G.] (4) The petition does not meet the requirements of CRC 7.550(b). Notwithstanding Administrator's fallacious I&A (assets on hand as of the date of death were not all cash and cash equivalents), her petition does not address what happened to the securities and all other items of personal property, including silver certificates, foreign currency or coins, and small books of illustrations transferred from the conservator. (Furthermore, her amended petition merely attached a copy of the conservator's assets on hand at the beginning of the conservator's final accounting, which preceded the date of Decedent's death.) (5) Administrator alleges in paragraph 3 of her amended petition that personal property was appraised at $13,551.00, but her own inadequate I&A filed on 1/12/23 (which should have been a Corrected I&A, as a prior, incomplete I&A had been filed) listed "ivory figurines" at $1,500, and "jewelry" at $11,816.00, which total $13,316.00. (6) A petition for final distribution must state specifically how the estate is to be distributed, including the net amount of cash (as of a date certain) distributable to each beneficiary. [CRC 7.651; LRSF 14.35 I.6.] Fractional interests alone are not sufficient. Please provide a revised proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 4/25/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (The I&A was entirely inadequate. An appearance will likely be required after procedural issues are addressed in order for the Court to determine if Administratorâs failure to file a proper I&A is waivable. The administrator was granted IAEA authority on 12/6/21 in the order appointing her; she did not qualify for and claim her Letters until 1/3/22.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SUN WAH MOON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/29/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Please file a FIFTH SUPPLEMENT to the petition that attaches a complete Attachment 8 to the petition (listing all heirs and their dates of death, if deceased) and including documents supporting how a party is an interested party. (2) It appears that Frank Toy Moon died testate, although his will has apparently not been probated. In Petitioner's first supplement, filed on 10/23/23, he disclosed that Frank was survived by at least one child, Michael David Moon. (3) The Probate Code Section 13100 affidavit attached to the [fourth] supplement filed on 3/8/24 claims that there is no real property in the estate of Frank Toy Moon, but the only asset in this estate is purported to be real property estimated at $340,000 as of 2/10/04, which has not yet been distributed. The affidavit is also otherwise quite incomplete, as there are purportedly multiple successors-in-interest to Frank Toy Moon, but only Petitioner signed it. (4) A Probate Code Section 13100 affidavit must be filed conventionally. [LRSF 14.59 C.] Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues by 4/19/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Petitioner is strongly urged to hire an attorney.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF PIK KAU LEE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/15/24. (No earlier availability.) 1. A Judicial Council form must not be used to supplement a pleading. [CRC 7.104(b)(2).] A supplement to a Petition for Letters of Administration must be prepared on pleading paper and must be signed and verified by the proposed personal representative. Merely correcting a copy of an already signed/verified and filed pleading is not adequate. 2. Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] Please provide courtesy copies of the petition and any other filed papers to the assigned examiner by 4/5/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Procedural issues on Lakhsmi Yabut's Petition: (1) Need re-notice. Notice failed to set forth that Petition offers a will to be admitted, in #4. Prob. Code sec. 8100. CRC 7.50. (2) Need re-publication of the notice. Publication failed to set forth that Petition seeks admission of a will. Prob. Code sec.8120. CRC 7.54. (3) Petition to set forth if the decedent's will is self-proving, in #3.f.(2). (4) Proposed order must be submitted Procedural issues on Will Contest by Depak Khiani: (A) Proof of service of Summons to Lakhsmi and Jennifer not on file. Prob. Code sec. 8250(a). The Will Contestant filed declaration regarding service of Summons. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROSA OSALVO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Procedural issues on Depak's Petition: (1) There have been two First Amended Petitions on file. 1/22/2024 and 1/26/2024. Need clarification if the later document must be the "Second" Amended Petition. (2) Proposed personal representative appears to be Administrator with Will Annexed not Executor because it is unknown if the purported lost will named himself as executor. (3) A copy of the lost will to be provided. It needs to be attached to Petition. Also, a copy of the lost will need to be submitted to the clerk's office for the purpose of admission. (4) Need allegation on the lost will pursuant to Prob. Code sec. 8223. Attachment 3F(3) alleges the will was not in the decedent's possession; however, petition does not provide any supportive facts or proof. (5) Notice of Petition to Administer Estate needs to be served on all persons titled. Prob. Code sec. 8110. The notice filed on 1/22/2024; yet it does not include the notice of direction of remote appearance. LRSF 14.1.C.6) (6) Petition to suggest an appropriate bond amount of $1,100,000. First Amended Petition filed 1/22/2024 requests $100,000 bond be ordered (#2.d.(2)); however, full bond of $1,100,000 is required. Prob. Code sec. 8482(a). If Petition seeks no IAEA, the court may require a minimum bond. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LUIS D. VIDALON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/15/2024. Petition is premature for the examiner's review. The only document on file is the petition [Form DE-221] itself. Additional information, including but not limited to the following must be filed two weeks prior for the examiner's review: 1. Notice of Hearing [DE-120]. See Prob. Code sec. 13655; 2. Notice of Directions for Remote Appearances and Access. See Local Rule 14.1.C.6). 3. Proposed Spousal Property Order [Form DE-226]. See Local Rule 14.7. 4. Petition itself instructs an attachment to be filed; however, no such attachments to the petition have been filed. (1) Attachment #7.a. describing the specific property subject to the request. See #7.a. (2) Attachment #7 setting forth supporting facts upon which the petitioner bases the allegation that the property identified in Attachment #7.a. See #7. Also, see Local Rule 14.26 in detail. (3) Attachment #7.b if Petition #1.b intentionally affirms what Petitioner seeks. See Prob. Code sec. 13650 and 13651 in detail. (4) Attachment #11 as petition affirms that the decedent died testate (with a will). If the allegation is accurate, Petition#4.c must mirror the information, too. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JUNGCHI SHIH
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. No appearance required. Status hearing on close of estate is set for 5/19/2025. The personal representative shall file either a petition for an order for final distribution or a petition for approval of report of status of administration pursuant to Prob. Code sec. 12200 et seq. two weeks before this date.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF PAUL D. BRIARD
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. Status of closing the estate will be set for 4/1/2025. A petition for final distribution or a report of status with a petition requesting additional time for estate administration must be filed at least two weeks before that status date. Appearance will be required on 4/1/2025 if no petition has been filed.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MOZELL BROOKS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/22/2024. The following issues to be cleared two weeks before: (1) Need allegation of the nature of the "FINANCIAL CODICIL" signed by the testator on 10/118/2023 whether it should be admitted as a codicil or an addendum to the will (i.e., schedule). Petition #3.f.(2) seeks that the will dated 11/01/2023 to be admitted. No codicil is offered. (2) Need further clarification of the date of the execution of the will. Page 5 of the will includes two different dates of execution (10/18/23 and 10/27/2023) by the testator plus the later date of subscription of the witnesses (11/1/2023). Also, the "FINANCIAL COCIDIL" was signed by the testator on 10/18/23 but notarized on 11/1/2023. Petition and the proposed order list the date of the will as 11/1/2023; however, the date of execution needs to be the date of the will. (3) Need information of all heirs at law. Petition #8 lists there is only one cousin survived the decedent. Petition #8 lists Yvonne Lewis is a surviving child of the decedent's aunt, Della Jones, Petition needs to identify all paternal and maternal sides of heirs, and each of them needs to be noticed, too. Prob. Code sec. 8110. CRC 7.52. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DAVID ALAN BOND
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Court to determine whether the presumption in Probate Code Section 6124 applies. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Rulings: Hearing for the sole purpose of admission of will. No appearance required. The bond must be fixed at $1,000,000 pursuant to Prob. Code sec. 8482(a), A status hearing on close of estate is set for 5/21/2025.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EVELYN JUNG WONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/6/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The original will does not appear to have been lodged or filed with the Court. (2) A proposed non-resident personal representative must file a declaration setting forth in detail the anticipated liabilities of the decedent and claims against the estate for the Court to determine the amount of the bond. [LRSF 14.16 F.] (3) Item 8 of a petition for probate must clearly identify who the trustee of the devisee trust is, and if the petitioner is the trustee, all trust beneficiaries must be listed in item 8 of the petition, must be clearly identified as such, and must receive notice. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 4/25/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Counsel might also wish to provide a revised proposed order that shows this petitioner's correct name and has the correct boxes checked and completed for a testate estate.)
